zh.lifeSummary: 當David Smith於1726年1月28日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波易亞斯坦時，他的父親Jesse R. Smith Sr.22歲，母親Sarah Higgins19歲。他於1746年6月16日與Sarah Ruggles在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德劍橋大學默里 米爾福德結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1783年在美國紐約州普特南卡梅爾享年57歲，埋葬在美國紐約州普特南卡梅爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当David Smith于1726年1月28日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波易亞斯坦时，他的父亲Jesse R. Smith Sr.22岁，母亲Sarah Higgins19岁。他于1746年6月16日与Sarah Ruggles在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德劍橋大學默里 米爾福德结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1783年在美国纽约州普特南卡梅爾享年57岁，埋葬在美国纽约州普特南卡梅爾。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

en.lifeSummary: When David Smith was born on 28 January 1726, in Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, his father, Jesse R. Smith Sr., was 22 and his mother, Sarah Higgins, was 19. He married Sarah Ruggles on 16 June 1746, in New Milford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1783, in Carmel, Putnam, New York, United States, at the age of 57, and was buried in Carmel, Putnam, New York, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque David Smith est né le 28 janvier 1726, à Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Jesse R. Smith Sr., avait 22 ans et sa mère, Sarah Higgins, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Sarah Ruggles le 16 juin 1746, à New Milford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1783, à Carmel, Putnam, New York, États-Unis, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Carmel, Putnam, New York, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als David Smith am 28. Januar 1726 in Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jesse R. Smith Sr. 22 und seine Mutter Sarah Higgins 19. Er hat am 16. Juni 1746 in New Milford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America Sarah Ruggles geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1783 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Carmel, Putnam, New York, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Carmel, Putnam, New York, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando David Smith è nato il il 28 gennaio 1726 nel Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, il suo padre, Jesse R. Smith Sr., aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Sarah Higgins, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Sarah Ruggles il 16 giugno 1746, nel New Milford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1783, nel Carmel, Putnam, New York, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolto a Carmel, Putnam, New York, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: David Smithが1726年1月28日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーバーンスタブルエアスサムで生まれた時，父Jesse R. Smith Sr.は22歳，母Sarah Higginsは19歳であった。1746年6月16日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードニュー ミルフォードでSarah Rugglesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1783年にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州プウトナムカーメルで57歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州プウトナムカーメルにて埋葬された。

ko.Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay,: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 바른스타블일 이스섬
ko.lifeSummary: David Smith(이)가 1726년 1월 28일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 바른스타블일 이스섬에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jesse R. Smith Sr.(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Sarah Higgins(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1746년 6월 16일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 뉴 미퍼드에서 Sarah Ruggles(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1783년 미국 뉴욕 주 풋남 카멀에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 뉴욕 주 풋남 카멀에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando David Smith nasceu em 28 de janeiro de 1726, em Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, Jesse R. Smith Sr., tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Higgins, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah Ruggles em 16 de junho de 1746, em New Milford, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1783, em Carmel, Putnam, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Carmel, Putnam, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда David Smith родился 28 января 1726 г. в местности эастам, барнстабл, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Jesse R. Smith Sr. было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Sarah Higgins, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Ruggles 16 июня 1746 г. в местности ню милфорд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1783 г. в местности кармел, путнам, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности кармел, путнам, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando David Smith nació el 28 de enero de 1726, en Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, Jesse R. Smith Sr., tenía 22 años y su mamá, Sarah Higgins, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Sarah Ruggles el 16 de junio de 1746, en New Milford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1783, en Carmel, Putnam, Nueva York, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultado en Carmel, Putnam, Nueva York, Estados Unidos.
